reflection.theme: The Tuesday in the Middle of May
reflection.quote: The most important thing in life is to learn how to give out love, and to let it come in.
reflection.reading:
Mid-May Tuesdays don't make highlight reels. The grass is finally green, the evenings stretch a little longer, and somewhere in your week there's a stretch of hours that don't ask anything dramatic of you. When I was drinking, this was the kind of day I'd waste — not in some big, cinematic way, but quietly. A few beers after work because the day was unremarkable. The unremarkable days were the dangerous ones.

What I've learned in eleven years is that a life worth showing up for is mostly built out of these afternoons. Not the trip to Whistler, not the wedding, not the skydive — those are the photos. The life is the Tuesday. It's making something for dinner instead of ordering it, calling someone back, going for the walk even though no one will know if you didn't. The texture of sobriety lives here, in the ordinary hours that used to feel like dead air.

Morrie was right that the work is learning to give love out and let it come back in. You can't do either when you're checked out. Presence isn't a mystical state — it's just being here, in this Tuesday, with whatever it actually contains. Five years from now you won't remember today specifically. But you'll be the person these Tuesdays made.
reflection.meditation: What would it look like to be fully present for one ordinary hour today?

affirmations: I am building my life out of ordinary days, not waiting for extraordinary ones.
Today I can be present for what's actually in front of me.
I am becoming someone I can live with.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Tuesday that asks nothing dramatic of me — just to show up. Grateful for the quiet ones who taught me that a life is built in these hours, not the loud ones. Grateful that I can taste my coffee, answer my phone, and remember what I said yesterday. That used to be impossible.
gratitude.closing: One ordinary day at a time.
